chemistry.scope_summary: Cross-electrophile coupling between aryl bromides and alkyl bromides using silyl radical activation

optimization_table.key_findings: Optimal conditions: 30 min irradiation, 4.2 wt% concentration, 100% lamp power, 50°C, giving 0.87 g/h productivity

reactor.type: HANU HX 15 oscillatory plug flow reactor

figures.key_information: Built on COSTA Technology, continuous processing in a linear plate flow reactor, oscillating flow regime, static mixing elements located in the process channel
figures.caption: Figure 1. Principles of the COSTA technology. The COSTA technology consists of 3 pillars: continuous processing in a  linear plate reactor by means of an oscillating flow regime and static mixing elements. The oscillating flow regime is  characterized by a net flow onto which a periodic flow is superimposed. 20

figures.trend: bell-shaped curves with peaks around θ = 1.0, showing frequency effect on residence time distribution
figures.caption: Figure 2. E-curves as function of pulsation amplitude (x 0 , % of maximum pulsation amplitude, 100% corresponds to 7 mm  center-to-peak amplitude, top left graph), frequency (top right) and flow rate (bottom).

figures.trend: bell-shaped curves with peak heights and positions varying by flow rate
figures.caption: Figure 2. E-curves as function of pulsation amplitude (x 0 , % of maximum pulsation amplitude, 100% corresponds to 7 mm  center-to-peak amplitude, top left graph), frequency (top right) and flow rate (bottom).

figures.trend: bell-shaped curves with peaks around θ = 1.0, amplitude decreases with increasing X_0
figures.caption: Figure 2. E-curves as function of pulsation amplitude (x 0 , % of maximum pulsation amplitude, 100% corresponds to 7 mm  center-to-peak amplitude, top left graph), frequency (top right) and flow rate (bottom).

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction times (6 hours) and low productivity in batch photochemistry
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Oscillatory plug flow enables reduced reaction time (30 min), increased concentration (0.21 M vs 0.01 M), higher productivity (0.87 g/h), and better mixing
translation_logic.what_changed: Temperature increased from 25°C to 50°C, concentration increased 21-fold from 0.01 M to 0.21 M, reaction time reduced from 360 min to 30 min, reactor changed to oscillatory plug flow with back pressure
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same photocatalyst system, same substrate stoichiometry, nitrogen atmosphere, similar yields (76% to 80%)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous processing with back pressure control and reduced solvent inventory

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing COSTA technology principles
E-curves showing residence time distribution effects

conflicts.resolution: Used 360 min for batch and 30 min for flow based on detailed text

notes: High quality data with detailed optimization information. Key limitation is missing specific flow rate values and some throughput calculations. BPR present based on back_pressure_bar value despite figure showing BPR_present=false.
